source: Compiled from Federal Student Aid, College Board CSS, institutional policies

fafsa.deadline: June 30 for federal aid; state/institutional deadlines vary (many are February/March)
fafsa.efc_replaced_by: SAI (Student Aid Index), effective starting 2024-2025 cycle
fafsa.incomeProtectionAllowance: Family of 4 with one in college: ~$32,000
commonMistakes: Missing priority deadline (many schools: Feb 1 or March 1)
Not filing as 'independent' when eligible (married, 24+, veteran, ward of court)
Forgetting to include non-custodial parent information for CSS Profile schools
Not reporting 529 savings correctly (reported as parent asset, not student asset)
Assuming too much income disqualifies you — FAFSA considers many factors
fafsa.appealProcess: Professional Judgment: Write to financial aid office explaining special circumstances (job loss, medical expenses, divorce, death). Include documentation. ~40-60% success rate at private schools.

cssProfile.keyDifference: CSS Profile is more comprehensive than FAFSA. Schools that use it generally have more institutional aid to give.
cssProfile.strategy: Some families are 'CSS-Profile-disadvantaged' (high home equity, divorced non-custodial parent with high income). Consider FAFSA-only schools as financial safeties.

meritAid.definition: Non-need-based scholarships awarded for academic, athletic, artistic, or other achievements
strategy: Target schools where your stats place you in the top 25% of admitted students
State flagships often have automatic merit thresholds (e.g., Alabama, Arizona State, Oklahoma)
Many T50-T100 schools offer significant merit to attract high-stat students away from T20
Negotiate: If you have a better offer from a comparable school, ask if they can match
Honors colleges at state schools: often provide near-Ivy experience at state school cost
Don't overlook full-ride competitions: Stamps, QuestBridge, Posse, Gates, Robertson

fullRidePrograms.name: Daniels Fund
fullRidePrograms.target: Students from CO, NM, UT, WY

needBlind.definition: Admission decision made without considering ability to pay
needBlind.note: Being need-blind doesn't guarantee meeting full need. Always run net price calculators.

needAware.definition: Financial situation may factor into admission, especially off waitlist or for borderline cases
needAware.strategy: At need-aware schools, full-pay students may have a slight admission advantage. Consider applying ED (binding) to maximize chances — schools want to lock in students who demonstrate top preference.
needAware.examples: Most LACs, many T30-T50 schools, all state schools for OOS students

netPriceCalculators.importance: EVERY school's website has a Net Price Calculator (NPC). Run it for your family before applying.
netPriceCalculators.strategy: Create a spreadsheet: school name, sticker price, estimated NPC, merit scholarship potential, total estimated cost
netPriceCalculators.surprises: Many families earning $100K-$200K are surprised to qualify for significant aid at wealthy private schools

examples.harvardFreeThreshold: Families earning < $85K/yr → free tuition + room & board
examples.stanfordFreeThreshold: Families earning < $100K/yr → free tuition; < $75K → free everything

529plans.overview: Tax-advantaged savings for education. Contributions grow tax-free; withdrawals for qualified expenses are tax-free.
529plans.fafsaImpact: Reported as parent asset (5.64% assessed) not student asset (20% assessed). Grandparent-owned 529s no longer counted as student income under simplified FAFSA.
529plans.strategy: Start early — even small monthly contributions compound significantly. $200/month from birth ≈ $80K by college.
529plans.superfunding: Gift tax rules allow 5-year gift tax averaging: contribute up to $90K at once ($18K × 5 years) without gift tax consequences.
